title: WIP: Add support for wand / wor net type.
body:
I'm working on synthesis of generated verilog code, which makes quite some use of the wor-nettype in conjunction with generate loops. As there's no easy way for me to change the code generator, I felt like it could be worth trying to add some support to yosys.

Basically, I came up with a possible implementation that solves my use case and wanted to get some feedback, on possible improvements or objections anyone might have.

The gist:
- The Verilog frontend adds a flag for nets that are wand/wor to their AstNodes when it encounters them.
- The genrtlil-pass only creates simple wires, which are tagged with a corresponding wand/wor attribute.
- The `hierarchy` pass
  - transforms these special wires into a `$reduce_or`/`$reduce_and` cell and connects its output to the wire created before
  - adds one input to the `$reduce_*`-cell for assignments to the nets in a module
  - adds one input to the `$reduce_*`-cell for connections to other cells in a module, when they are outputs

Current limitations:
- I only added support for 1-bit wide wires of type wand/wor

Thank you for any feedback.
